commit.message:
chat: fix body tag leaking into rendered markdown when content ends with code block (#309336)

* chat: fix body tag leaking into rendered markdown when content ends with code block

When markdown content ends with a fenced code block, the closing backticks
were immediately followed by </body> on the same line, so CommonMark didn't
recognize the fence as closed. Add \n\n before </body> to ensure proper
parsing. Add regression test.

(Written by Copilot)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: delete stale snapshots for regeneration

The extra \n\n before </body> changes rendered HTML output slightly,
so snapshot baselines need to be regenerated by CI.

(Written by Copilot)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: regenerate snapshot baselines for body tag fix

Update snapshot files to reflect the extra \n\n before </body> in the
markdown template, which adds trailing whitespace before the closing
wrapper div in rendered HTML output.

(Written by Copilot)

- ChatViewModel: load E4B on GPU before FunctionGemma — full memory
  headroom during GPU kernel compilation peak (~4-5GB transient)
- ChatViewModel/initEngineWhenReady: release EmbeddingGemma only before
  E4B init; FG no longer released (E4B-first makes it safe)
- ActionsViewModel: remove unused InferenceEngine injection

  phantom bottom padding below chat input

  skills section and roadmap to reflect Resident Agent Architecture

  Tier 3 — see issues #218 #219 #220)

* fix(#221): address code review — mutex double-init, FG idempotency, location permission guard

- Wrap initEngineWhenReady() in gemma4InitMutex to prevent concurrent
  sendMessage() from racing during the ~20s GPU init window. Eliminates
  the double-close of EmbeddingGemma and potential 3.4GB GPU engine orphan.
- Add initMutex to FunctionGemmaRouter.initialize() so concurrent calls
  from ActionsViewModel and ChatViewModel serialize safely and are idempotent.
- Guard location permission request in MainActivity with checkSelfPermission —
  prevents the dialog firing on every onCreate() (rotation, process restart).

commit.message:
Implementar base de datos Room para caché persistente offline del mapa y la red de calles (#13)

* Add RoadNetworkCache and VM integration

Introduce a persistent RoadNetworkCache (disk-based, TTL, LRU per ~2km cells) and integrate it into WorldMapViewModel via a Context-aware Factory. Configure osmdroid caching and parameters in MainActivity and use the ViewModel.Factory when creating the view model. Improve OverpassRepository: expand fetch radius to 2000m, increase read timeout, better HTTP error handling, URLEncoding, and parsing optimizations. Update WorldMapViewModel to prefer cache on startup, save downloads to cache, add refetch cooldown/thresholds, and restructure the game loop. UI refinements in WorldMapScreen: use the Factory for the ViewModel, marker caching, smoother zoom animations, WebView JS calls formatting, layout/style polish, and loading/zoom behavior. Also add zoom constants in WorldMapState.

* Migrate road cache to Room; add tile cache

Replace the old file/JSON road-network cache with a Room-backed implementation and add a new per-provider tile cache.

- RoadNetworkCache: switched from file-based JSON to Room (uses RoadZone/RoadWay/RoadNode entities and RoadNetworkDao). Implements TTL checks, LRU eviction via DAO, atomic insertZoneWithData transaction, and reconstitution of MapWay from DB rows.
- Added PowDatabase (Room) created under filesDir so DB is persistent and excluded from external cleaners.
- Added TileCache: SQLite-backed tile storage per provider with TTL and simple LRU eviction, stored under filesDir as provider.mbtiles.
- Added Room entities (RoadEntities.kt) and DAO (RoadNetworkDao.kt) to support the new cache.
- MainMenuScreen: UI overhaul (layout tweaks, settings panel, provider selector) and now passes chosen MapProvider when navigating to the map.
- MainActivity: OSMDroid configuration simplified (uses app filesDir, explicit cache params), navigation transitions cleaned, and world map provider is applied to WorldMapViewModel; minor permission/location call formatting cleaned up.
- build.gradle.kts: added ksp plugin and Room dependencies (runtime/ktx + ksp compiler), reorganized some dependencies and simplified compileSdk setting.

These changes centralize persistent map data in Room/SQLite for reliability and atomicity, add a robust tile cache, and let users choose map providers via the main menu.

* Fix review issues: composite PK, TileCache context, SHA-256 URL key, CacheStatusWidget, DAO location, .gitignore

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gabrielhuav/PolitecnicoOpenWorld/sessions/89d7cab6-4830-4f6b-a113-948e9ad1a9c4

Co-authored-by: gabrielhuav <71236850+gabrielhuav@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use application context in TileCache

Store the application context (appContext) in TileCache instead of a generic Context to avoid leaking activity contexts. Updated tilesDir initialization and the TileDatabase SQLiteOpenHelper to use appContext/applicationContext and made the constructor parameter private.

* Migrate tile cache to Room (MapTileEntity/DAO)

Replace the old file/SQLite-based TileCache with a Room-backed implementation: add MapTileEntity and MapTileDao, refactor TileCache to use MapTileDao (insert/get/count/deleteOldest), and remove the custom SQLiteOpenHelper logic. Improve CachingWebViewClient to normalize tile URLs, hash the normalized URL for cache keys, only intercept GET requests, refine MIME guessing and logging, and download using the original URL. Wire the new DAO into PowDatabase (add mapTileDao, include MapTileEntity) and bump DB version to 3; update WorldMapViewModel to obtain the mapTileDao and pass it into TileCache. Includes LRU eviction via DAO when MAX_TILES_PER_PROVIDER is exceeded.

* Add cache & FPS widget toggles and sync UI

Introduce showCacheWidget and showFpsWidget state and controls across the main menu and world map. MainMenuState and WorldMapState gain new flags, MainMenuViewModel exposes toggleCacheWidget/toggleFpsWidget and MainMenu UI now lets users enable/disable cache and FPS widgets before starting. MainActivity navigation callback updated to pass cache/fps choices to WorldMapViewModel, and WorldMapViewModel adds update/toggle functions to apply them. WorldMapScreen now computes real-time FPS (when enabled) and shows/hides diagnostic widgets (cache status and FPS) with animated visibility; also contains small UI/formatting cleanups. Function signatures and some composables were adjusted to wire the new options end-to-end.

* Fix composite PK for tiles, empty cache guard, encoding fix, remove redundant withContext

commit.message:
Fix Windows CI build failures by making spawn tests Unix-only

The tests for SpawnTool path resolution use 'ls' and absolute paths like
'/bin/bash' which are POSIX-specific and don't exist on Windows. These
tests were failing on the Windows CI runner.

Fixed by adding #[cfg(unix)] to both:
- test_spawn_validation
- test_spawn_path_resolution

This allows these tests to be skipped on Windows while still running on
Unix-like systems (Linux, macOS). All 82 tests pass locally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Playwright CLI provenance verification for tag format change (#16087)

* Fix Playwright CLI provenance verification for tag format change

@playwright/cli 0.1.7 changed their git tag naming convention from
'v0.1.7' to '0.1.7' (dropped the 'v' prefix). This caused our
provenance verification to fail with WorkflowRefMismatch.

- Accept both tag formats (with and without 'v' prefix) in the
  validateWorkflowRef callback for forward/backward compatibility
- Add test verifying both tag formats pass validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add E2E test for aspire new with agent init and Playwright install

Exercises the full aspire new → agent init → Playwright CLI install
flow end-to-end, verifying no provenance verification errors appear.
This is the primary regression test for tag format changes in upstream
@playwright/cli releases.

The test accepts the agent init prompt (instead of declining), selects
Playwright CLI during skill selection, and fails fast if a provenance
verification error is detected during npm package installation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: model version management — admin assigns versions, Jetson auto-builds engines

Dashboard (DevicesPage):
- Fetch active model versions per leaf_type from model_registry
- Model version selector per leaf_type in device edit form
- model_versions included in desired_config on save
- Display model versions + engine status (ready/building/error) in device table
- Config sync check ignores Jetson-added fields (engine_status, applied_model_versions)

Jetson (sync_engine):
- Detect model_versions change in polled desired_config
- Auto-build: check engine cache → download ONNX → trtexec --fp16 → hot-swap
- Exact version ONNX download (REST query by leaf_type+version with RPC fallback)
- Upload built engines to Storage cache for reuse by other devices
- Report engine_status + applied_model_versions in reported_config
- Re-report config on build completion/failure so dashboard updates
- Thread-safe config persistence (_config_lock protects read-modify-write)
- get_model_version() API for correct version tracking in predictions

Jetson (inference):
- InferenceWorkerPool.hot_swap() — queue-based swap on CUDA worker thread
- _do_hot_swap: load new engine BEFORE destroying old (graceful degradation)
- 120s timeout for slow Jetson devices

Docs:
- jetson_deployment_guide: section 9.5 Model Version Management
- admin_dashboard_manual: updated Device Fleet Tab
- project_context: model version management architecture
